title: Repository Permission Changes
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="github:audit" action IN ("repo.update","team.add_repository","repo.access")
| where visibility="public" OR permission IN ("admin","maintain")
| table _time, actor, repo, action, permission, visibility
description: Team `maintain`/`admin` grants and visibility changes to `public` are higher risk than member adds (UC-12.1.5).
value: Team `maintain`/`admin` grants and visibility changes to `public` are higher risk than member adds (UC-12.1.5).
implementation: Alert on public visibility toggles and admin grants. Quarterly access review export.
visualization: Table (high-risk changes), Timeline, Single value (public repos).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: GitHub audit log.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `repo.update`, `team.add_repository`, `org.update_member`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Alert on public visibility toggles and admin grants. Quarterly access review export.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="github:audit" action IN ("repo.update","team.add_repository","repo.access")
| where visibility="public" OR permission IN ("admin","maintain")
| table _time, actor, repo, action, permission, visibility
```

Understanding this SPL

**Repository Permission Changes** — Team `maintain`/`admin` grants and visibility changes to `public` are higher risk than member adds (UC-12.1.5).

Documented **Data sources**: `repo.update`, `team.add_repository`, `org.update_member`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): GitHub audit log. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: github:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="github:audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where visibility="public" OR permission IN ("admin","maintain")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Repository Permission Changes**): table _time, actor, repo, action, permission, visibility


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GitHub audit log. Verify field extractions and index permissions.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (high-risk changes), Timeline, Single value (public repos).
grandmaExplanation: We watch who can change repository settings and membership so privilege drift does not slip past your team.
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Change.All_Changes
  by All_Changes.user All_Changes.object All_Changes.action span=1h
| sort -count
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration on Change.All_Changes before using this tstats search.
knownFalsePositives: Activity shifts during holidays, release freezes, or vendor maintenance; confirm against your change calendar.
